title: Lock Driver Availability Toggle When Accepted Trip Exists

requirements.text: In the backend, add a guard to the `updateAvailability` (or equivalent) function: if the calling driver has any trip with status `accepted`, reject any attempt to set availability to `false` (Unavailable) and return a descriptive error message such as 'Cannot set Unavailable while you have an accepted trip.'

quotes: Block backend updates that try to set Unavailable while an accepted trip exists (return a clear error)

acceptanceCriteria: Calling setAvailability(false) while the driver has an accepted trip returns an error variant with a clear message.
Calling setAvailability(true) or setAvailability(false) when no accepted trip exists succeeds as before.
No changes are made to trip acceptance, completion, or any other backend function.

requirements.text: On the Driver Dashboard frontend, derive a boolean `hasAcceptedTrip` by checking whether the driver's 'My Accepted Trips' list contains any trip with status `accepted`. When `hasAcceptedTrip` is true: (a) force the displayed availability to Available/true regardless of the stored value, (b) render the availability toggle in a disabled/greyed-out state so the driver cannot interact with it, and (c) display helper text directly beneath the toggle reading: 'Availability is locked while you have an accepted trip.'

quotes: Show helper text: "Availability is locked while you have an accepted trip."

acceptanceCriteria: When the driver has at least one accepted trip, the availability toggle is visually disabled (greyed out) and cannot be clicked.
The toggle displays Available (true) regardless of the driver's last saved value while any accepted trip exists.
Helper text 'Availability is locked while you have an accepted trip.' is visible beneath the toggle when locked.
Helper text is hidden and toggle is enabled when no accepted trips exist.
No changes are made to the Available Trips section, My Accepted Trips section layout, or any customer/admin UI.

requirements.text: When the driver clicks 'Mark as Completed' and the trip transitions out of `accepted` status (i.e., the accepted trips list becomes empty or no accepted trips remain), re-enable the availability toggle and restore it to the driver's last persisted availability value from their profile.

quotes: When driver clicks 'Mark as Completed' and the trip becomes completed (or removed from 'My Accepted Trips'): Re-enable the availability toggle, Restore the driver's last saved availability value

acceptanceCriteria: After marking a trip as completed, if no other accepted trips remain, the availability toggle becomes interactive again.
The toggle reflects the driver's last saved availability value (from their profile) after unlocking.
If the driver had multiple accepted trips and only one is completed, the toggle remains locked until all accepted trips are resolved.
No changes are made to the Mark as Completed mutation logic or the DriverTripList layout.

requirements.text: Add a post-build checklist comment block (as a visible UI element or inline code comment) confirming the two verification steps: (1) Accept trip → toggle is disabled and availability shows Available; (2) Complete trip → toggle is re-enabled.

quotes: Add a short post-build checklist: Accept trip → toggle disabled and availability shows Available, Complete trip → toggle enabled again

acceptanceCriteria: A post-build checklist with the two steps is either rendered as a visible UI note on the Driver Dashboard (e.g., a small dev/QA callout) or included as a clearly labeled comment in the relevant component file.
The checklist does not affect production UI for end users if rendered only as a code comment.

constraints: Do not change trip acceptance logic (acceptTrip mutation or backend function).
Do not modify the customer booking form or CustomerDashboard.
Do not modify admin pages, role locking, or login routing.
Do not change the layout or content of the Available Trips or My Accepted Trips sections.
Scope all frontend changes exclusively to the Driver Dashboard and its sub-components (availability toggle area).
Do not edit any file listed under frontend.immutablePaths.
nonGoals: Changing availability lock behavior for any role other than driver.
Modifying trip status transitions beyond what is needed for the unlock trigger.
Adding new trip statuses or modifying the existing status enum.
Admin override of driver availability lock.
